content.problemStatement:
Chef gave you the headheadhead of a sorted linked list which may contain duplicate values. You've to remove all the duplicate values and return the sorted linked list such that it does not contain any duplicates.


Input Format

First-line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of every test case contains an integer NNN - the length of array.
The second line of every test case contains NNN integers - A1,A2,..,AN A_1,A_2,..,A_NA1​,A2​,..,AN​ denoting the integers in the array.
It is guaranteed that the array is sorted
You don't need to read or print anything. Just complete the function removeDuplicates() which takes the head of the linked list as input.


Output Format
Return the head of the sorted linked list without duplicates.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 5·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the linked list. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN in non‑decreasing order.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output the values of the linked list after removing all duplicate nodes. Print the values in a single line separated by a single space. Print a newline after each test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each test case is a sorted list. Duplicates are removed by keeping only the first occurrence of each value. For example, the first list [6,15,22,22] becomes [6,15,22].
The list contains duplicates of 1 and 3. After removal only distinct values remain.
A single‑element list has no duplicates, so it is printed unchanged.

solution.approach: Because the list is already sorted, duplicate values appear consecutively. Traverse the list with a single pointer. For each node, compare its value with the next node's value. If they are equal, bypass the next node (current->next = current->next->next) and delete it (or let the garbage collector free it). Otherwise move the pointer forward. This runs in O(N) time and O(1) extra space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the list. Overall O(∑N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (the list itself is reused).
